questions.text: The plural form of the word 'medium' is:
questions.explanation: Media describes the various ways through which we communicate in society.

questions.text: TV, radio, and newspapers are called 'mass media' because:
options: They reach millions of people across the world
They are owned by the government
They use a lot of electricity
questions.explanation: Because they reach 'masses' of people, they are categorized as mass media.

questions.text: What is the main source of income for most mass media outlets?
questions.explanation: Mass media is constantly thinking of ways to make money, and advertisements are the most effective method.

options: It is free from government control
No one should control its coverage of news
It can write whatever it wants without checking facts
questions.explanation: An independent media is essential in a democracy to provide reliable and balanced information.

questions.text: When the government prevents the media from showing or publishing certain items, it is called:
questions.explanation: There have been periods in Indian history, like the Emergency (1975-1977), when the government censored the media.

options: Discusses all points of view of a story
questions.explanation: It leaves it to the readers to make up their own minds after presenting all sides.

questions.text: The phrase 'setting the agenda' means that the media:
options: Determines which stories are important enough to be news
questions.explanation: By focusing on particular issues, the media influences our thoughts and feelings on those subjects.

questions.text: 'Khabar Lahriya' is a local newspaper run by eight Dalit women in:
questions.explanation: This newspaper is written in the local Bundeli dialect and covers issues relevant to the local community.

questions.text: Which technology allows mass media to reach a global audience via satellites and cables?
questions.explanation: Satellites have enabled us to view news and entertainment from around the world instantly.

questions.text: Social advertising refers to advertisements made by the government or private agencies that have a:
questions.explanation: Examples include ads regarding road safety or the importance of pulse polio drops.
